ground_truth.title: Gardener shares impressive before-and-after photos 4 years after transforming boring yard: 'Love it'
ground_truth.author: Lily Crowder
ground_truth.main_content: Upgrading your yard from drab monoculture grass to native plant options is not only an eco-friendly move but also an aesthetically amazing one. A recent Reddit user's post showcases a remarkable transformation shared on the r/gardening subreddit. The poster shared before-and-after pictures of their yard transformation spanning 4 years. What was once a plain grassy yard became a haven for a variety of plants and garden installations. Besides being boring, monoculture grass lawns are highly resource-dependent, needing excessive water or pesticides to stay green. Many are turning to native lawns, seeking out grasses or plants native to their area. With options like clover or buffalo grass, yards can be rewilded with ease, saving time and money. Native plants benefit pollinators and the entire food chain. Another easy addition is xeriscaping, a natural landscaping method that conserves water. Commenters on the post voiced their approval, with one user saying "From house to home, love it!" and another noting "Well done! It's now your haven. Crazy how much bigger, cozier and more interesting a garden looks once you get it planted up!"
with: Upgrading your yard from drab monoculture grass to native plant options is not only an eco-friendly move but also an aesthetically amazing one.
What was once a plain grassy yard became a haven for a variety of plants and other garden installations.
Besides being plain boring, monoculture grass lawns are often highly resource-dependent, needing excessive water or pesticides to stay green.
Native plants are great for pollinators, since, unlike invasive or artificial options, they benefit surrounding organisms and thus the entire food chain.
"Well done! It's now your haven. I understand. We've done the same at our house."
